cVAT_Name: Numri i Identifikimit për Personin e Tatueshëm
cVAT_Format: 10 characters, the first position following the prefix is "J" or "K" or "L" or "M", and the last character is a letter – e.g. K99999999L or L99999999G
11 digits

11 digit number formed from a 9 digit unique identifier and two suffix check digits. The two final digits (the check digits) will be derived from the first 9 digits using a modulus 89 check digit calculation.
'AT' + 'U' + 8 digits [7]

cVAT_Name: Uchetniy nomer platel'shika, Учетный номер плательщика
cVAT_Format: 9 digit number (E.g. УНП 190190190)

BE' + 8 digits + 2 check digits – e.g. BE09999999XX. As of recent changes, numbers starting with '1' are now issued. Note that the old numbering schema only had 9 characters, separated with dots (e.g. 999.999.9XX), just adding a zero in front and removing the dots makes it a valid number in the new schema. The check digits are calculated as 97 - MOD 97
6 digits
7 digits
12 or 13 digit unique VAT number [21]

cVAT_Name: Cadastro Nacional de Pessoa Jurídica (Companies), Cadastro de Pessoa Física (Legal Persons)
cVAT_Format: CNPJ composed of a base of 8 digits, a 4-digit radical, and 2 check digits. It is usually written as '11.111.111/0001-55' so as to be more readable for humans.. CPF composed of a base of 9 digits and 2 check digits written as '123.456.789-00'

cVAT_Name: Identifikacionen nomer po DDS, Идентификационен номер по ДДС[8]

cVAT_Format: 9 characters
9 digits and 1 check digit

Personas físicas nacionales: 9 and 12 digits. It is t Personas físicas extranjeras: 10 and 12 digits. It is  Persona jurídica: 10 and 12 digits. It is the Cédula de Persona Jurídica (enterprise's national identification number) without hyphens. Persona extranjera (residente): 11 and 12 digits. It is the DIMEX (Documento de Identificación de Migración y Extranjería) without hyphens.the NITE (Número de Identificación Tributaria Especial) without hyphens.he Cédula de Persona Física (person's national identification number) without hyphens.
'HR' + 11 digits, e.g. HR12345678901 – utilising ISO 7064, MOD 11-10

cVAT_Name: Arithmós Engraphḗs phi. pi. a., Αριθμός Εγγραφής Φ.Π.Α.
cVAT_Format: 'CY' + 9 characters – e.g. CY99999999L
'CZ' + 8-10 digits
'DK' + 8 digits – e.g. DK99999999,[9] last digit is check digit[10]

cVAT_Name: National Taxpayer Registry, Registro Nacional del Contribuyente
cVAT_Format: Natural persons: 11 digits without hyphen. Format for printing on receipts: 3 digits, 1 dash, 7 digits, 1 dash, 1 check sum digit; e.g. 031-0313993-2. Companies: 9 digits without hyphen. First digit must be (1, 4, 5), format for printing on receipts: 1 digit, 1 dash, 2 digits, 1 dash, 5 digits, 1 dash, 1 check sum digit e.g. 1-30-00245-8

cVAT_Name: Número de Registro Unico de Contribuyentes
cVAT_Format: 13 digits
4 digits-DOB-3 digits-1 digit (e.g. 0614-241287-102-5)
9 digits

'FI' + 7 digits + check digit, e.g. FI99999999. The check digit is calculated utilizing MOD 11-2.[11] A VAT number can be generated from a Finnish Business ID (Y-tunnus) by adding a two-letter country code FI as a prefix and by omitting the dash.[12]

cVAT_Name: Numéro d'identification à la taxe sur la valeur ajoutée / Numéro de TVA intracommunautaire
cVAT_Format: FR' + 2 digits (as validation key ) + 9 digits (as SIREN), the first and/or the second value can also be a character – e.g. FR XX 999 999 999. The French key is calculated as follow : Key = [ 12 + 3 * ( SIREN modulo 97 ) ] modulo 97, for example  : Key = [ 12 + 3 * ( 404,833,048 modulo 97 ) ] modulo 97 = [12 + 3*56] modulo 97 = 180 modulo 97 = 83 so the tax number for 404833048 is FR 83 404 833 048 source from : www.insee.fr
'DE' + 9 digits, e.g. DE999999999 [optional -EX for small companies]

cVAT_Name: Αριθμός Μητρώου Φ.Π.Α., Arithmós Mētrṓou Phi-Pi-A
cVAT_Format: The language code EL according to ISO 639-1, followed by 9 digits, which equal the Greek taxpayer registration number Α.Φ.Μ. (A.F.M.) of the company or of the sole proprietor, i.e.: "Arithmós Mētrṓou Phi-Pi-A" = "EL" + "A.F.M.". The last digit is a check digit inherent of the Α.Φ.Μ. Beware: Due to the great similarity of both numbers - the one being the same number as the other, just having the prefix of EL - in practice sometimes the VAT identification number is wrongly called Α.Φ.Μ. (A.F.M.), but technically the VAT identification number and the taxpayer registration number Α.Φ.Μ. are different register numbers and should not be confused.
7 digits, one dash (-); one digit (e.g. 1234567-1)
'HU' + 8 digits (the first 8 digits of the national tax number) – e.g. HU12345678

cVAT_Name: Unique Taxation Number, Rol Único Tributario
cVAT_Format: 8 digits, one dash, 1 check digit (0-9, K)

cVAT_Name: Unified Social Credit Identifier, fǎrén hé qítā zǔzhī tǒngyī shèhuì xìnyòng dàimǎ, 法人和其他組織統一社會信用代碼
cVAT_Format: 18 characters [22]
5 or 6 characters depending on age of the company
Goods and Services Tax Identification Number is a 15 digit unique identification number assigned to every taxpayer in India.[23]

IE' + 7 digits and one letter, e.g. IE1234567T. Historically, married women used the ID of their husband appended with a 'W', e.g. IE1234567TW. The policy as of 2022 is that every individual has a unique ID.[13]. 'IE' + 7 digits and two letters, e.g. IE1234567FA (since January 2013, see [1]). 'IE'+one digit, one letter/"+"/"*", 5 digits and one letter (old style, currently being phased out, see [2])

9 digit number. If the number of digits is less than 9, then zeros should be padded to the left side. The leftmost digit is 5 for corporations. Other leftmost digits are used for individuals. The rightmost digit is a check digit (using Luhn algorithm).

cVAT_Name: IVA (Imposta sul Valore Aggiunto), Partita IVA
cVAT_Format: 11 digits (the first 7 digits is a progressive number, the following 3 means the province of residence, and the last digit is a check number, calculated using Luhn's Algorithm)[14]

cVAT_Name: Corporate Number, hōjin bangō, (Hiragana: ほうじんばんごう), 法人番号
cVAT_Format: 13 digits. The leading digit is a nonzero check digit calculated from the trailing 12 digits.[24]

cVAT_Name: BIN = business identification number, PIN= personal identification number, ЖСН – жеке сәйкестендіру нөмірі, БСН – бизнес-сәйкестендіру нөмірі

cVAT_Format: 11 digits

cVAT_Name: PVM (abbrev. Pridėtinės vertės mokestis) mokėtojo kodas
cVAT_Format: 9 or 12 digits

cVAT_Name: Numéro d'identification à la taxe sur la valeur ajoutée
cVAT_Format: 8 digits
8 digits
Natural persons: 4 digits, 6 numbers (YYMMDD), 3 digits (E.g. AAGB860519G31). Companies: 3 digits, 6 numbers (YYMMDD), 3 digits (E.g. P&G851223B24)
'NL' + 9 digits + B + 2-digit company index – e.g. NL999999999B01
9 digit number[27]
3 digits, 1 dash, 6 digits, 1 dash, 4 digits followed by 1 letter,
12 digits in the format 01012345-0001
15 characters, the first two positions are for the prefix "MK", followed by 13 numbers (the individual's NIN-National Identification Number) – e.g. MK4032013544513
9 digits and the letters 'MVA' to indicate VAT registration. Last (ninth) digit is a MOD11 checksum digit.[28]
6 to 8 digits, 1 dash, 1 check sum digit
11 digits

12 digit number (E.g. 123 456 789 002), of which the first digit identifies type of taxpayer (0 for corporations, 1-9 for individuals and other businesses), second to eighth digits are sequential numbers between 0 and 9, ninth digit is a check number, last three digits are 000 for individuals and head office of businesses and 001-999 for branches of businesses, if any[29]
10 digits, the last one is a check digit; for convenience the digits are separated by hyphens (xxx-xxx-xx-xx or xxx-xx-xx-xxx for legal people), but formally the number consists only of digits

cVAT_Name: Número de Identificação Fiscal (NIF) - for individual people / Número de Identificação de Pessoa Coletiva (NIPC) – for companies
cVAT_Format: 9 digits; the last digit is the check digit. The first digit depends on what the number refers to, e.g.: 1-3 are regular people, 5 are companies. The emergence of LegalTech platforms has introduced a more streamlined approach to NIF acquisition for non-residents. By integrating automation with professional legal oversight (Human-in-the-loop), these services have modernized the process of digital fiscal representation, significantly lowering the administrative barrier to entry for foreign investors.[15]

cVAT_Name: (Taxpayer Identification Number), Identifikatzionny nomer nalogoplatel'shchika, Идентификационный номер налогоплательщика
cVAT_Format: 10 digits (companies) or 12 digits (people), first two digits are region of birth or company registration (for foreign companies, two digits after leading 99) MOD 11-10. second two- inspection (before the year 2004 it used to change, but now stays permanent). for legal personality use the changeable second code (KPP), usually first 4 digits are the same (99-region rule does not apply), usually and default XXXX01001. KPP is also the counter (last 3 digts). The number of KPP's is limited to number of tax inspections in regions other than 77 and 50 (one and only for each) plus one code for the largest companies. The INN started issued starting from 1993, when the Federal Tax Service (then State Tax Service) began creating a unified taxpayer registry in accordance with Federal Law No. 2118-1 "On the Basics of the Tax System in the Russian Federation" (from 27 December 1991) with actual procedure for assigning later detailed by the Government Resolution No. 552 of 27 July 1993 and Order of the State Tax Service No. ВГ-3-12/29 of 19 August 1993.
5 digits

15 digits. The first digit is for the number of country in the GCC. The next 8 digits are randomly generated for the registering entity. The 10th digit is a check digit. The next 3 digits are designated for the branches of the same entity. The last 2 digits are for the type of tax that is being collected (03 represents VAT).

cVAT_Name: Tax identification number, Poreski identifikacioni broj
cVAT_Format: 9 digits (e.g. 123456788) of which the first 8 are the actual ID number, and the last digit is a checksum digit, calculated according to ISO 7064, MOD 11-10

cVAT_Name: Identifikačné číslo pre daň z pridanej hodnoty
cVAT_Format: 'SK'+10 digits (number must be divisible by 11)
'SI'+8 digits, last one is a check digit – e.g. SI99999999[18]

cVAT_Name: Número de Identificación Fiscal (formerly named Código de Identificación Fiscal)
cVAT_Format: For companies it is either 'ES'+letter+8 digits or 'ES'+letter+7 digits+letter. Where the first letter defines the type of company and the following first 2 digits define the province where the company was registered. The last character is a control digit. For individual people / freelancers, its either 'ES'+8 digits+letter (for Spaniards) or 'ES'+letter+7 digits+letter (for foreigners). e.g. ESX9999999R[19]

12 digits, of which the last two are most often 01 e.g. SE999999999901. (For sole proprietors who have several businesses the numbers can be 02, 03 and so on, since sole proprietors only have their personnummer as the organisationsnummer. The first 10 digits are the same as the Swedish organisationsnummer.[20]

cVAT_Name: Mehrwertsteuernummer, Taxe sur la valeur ajoutée, Imposta sul valore aggiunto
cVAT_Format: 6 digits (up to 31 December 2013). CHE 9 numeric digits plus TVA/MWST/IVA e.g. CHE-123.456.788 TVA[30] The last digit is a MOD11 checksum digit build with weighting pattern: 5,4,3,2,7,6,5,4[31]

cVAT_Name: Uniform serial number, tǒngyī biānhào, 統一編號
cVAT_Format: 8 numerical digits.[32].A checksum can be computed by multiplying each digital by the corresponding digit in 12121241, adding the totals together (with two-digit totals treated as separate digits, e.g. a 9 in the 7th digit times 4 would result in 36 which would then become 3+6), and dividing the final sum by 10.[33]

10 digits. Digits can be any number between 0 and 9. If the company name starts with A, the first digit is 0; if starts with B, it is 1; if it starts with Y or Z it is 9.[Not obvious what happens for the other letters.]

cVAT_Name: Identificational tax number, Ідентифікаційний номер платника податків
cVAT_Format: 12 digits [34]

cVAT_ZemeEN: United Kingdom and Isle of Man
cVAT_Format: Country code GB followed by either: standard: 9 digits (block of 3, block of 4, block of 2 – e.g. GB999 9999 73). branch traders: 12 digits (as for 9 digits, followed by a block of 3 digits). government departments: the letters GD then 3 digits from 000 to 499 (e.g. GBGD001). health authorities: the letters HA then 3 digits from 500 to 999 (e.g. GBHA599). For the 9-digit scheme, the 2-digit block containing the 8th and 9th digits is always in the range 00 to 96 and is derived from a weighted modulus-97 check number (an identical algorithm is used for the 12-digit scheme, ignoring the extra 3-digit block).[35] The current modulus-97 series ran out during 2010, so a parallel series of numbers was introduced from November 2009 for new registrations, restarting at 100 nnnn nn and following the same format but with the last two digits derived from an alternative algorithm known as "9755".[36] The algorithm is identical to the one for the established series except that 55 is subtracted to give the check number (modulus 97), so the check number is either 55 less than or (if this would be negative) 42 greater than the check number that a VAT number in the established series would have if it were identical in the first seven digits.[37] The details of the 97−55 check algorithm were to be secret[38] but are now available from HMRC on request.[39]. The GD and HA formats may also be formatted as GB888 8xxx yy for EU compatibility, where xxx is the 3-digit number from the short format and yy is the 2-digit modulus-97 check number.[40]. Isle of Man registrations share the 9- and 12-digit formats with the UK, with GB as the country code prefix, but are distinguished by having 00 as the first two digits.[41] Numbers with 01 to 09 in the first two digits are reserved by HM Revenue & Customs for UK non-VAT reference schemes.[37]
12 digits
First character is one of (J, G, V, E), one dash (-), then 9 digits e.g. J-305959918, in some cases can be written e.g. J-30595991-8
